name: Salesforce
one_liner: Market-leading cloud CRM platform for sales, service, and marketing that exposes customer data and business objects through an extensive family of platform APIs.
primary_auth: OAuth2 authorization code flow via a Connected App / Externa
main_blocker: None; only caveat is per-org OAuth Connected App setup and that Professional edition customer orgs need a paid API add-on.

name: HubSpot
one_liner: HubSpot is a cloud CRM and inbound marketing/sales/service platform covering contacts, deals, tickets, marketing campaigns, and CMS content.
main_blocker: None

name: Pipedrive
one_liner: Sales-focused CRM for managing pipelines, deals, contacts, and activities.
main_blocker: None

name: Attio
one_liner: Attio is a modern, flexible-data-model CRM for managing people, companies, deals, and pipelines.
main_blocker: None

name: Twenty
one_liner: Open-source CRM (AGPL, self-hosted or managed cloud) for managing people, companies, opportunities, and custom objects, with built-in AI agents and workflows.
main_blocker: None; the only nuance is that endpoints are generated per workspace schema, so a toolkit should use schema/metadata discovery rather than a fixed endpoint list.

name: Podio
one_liner: Progress Podio is a customizable work-management and collaboration platform (often used as a low-code CRM) where teams build 'apps' of structured items in workspaces.
main_blocker: None

name: Zoho CRM
one_liner: Cloud CRM platform for managing leads, contacts, accounts, deals, and sales automation within the Zoho suite.
primary_auth: OAuth2 authorization-code grant with refresh tokens
main_blocker: None (free-edition cap of 5,000 API credits/day is the only constraint for heavy testing)

name: Close
one_liner: Close is a sales-focused CRM with built-in calling, email, and SMS that helps SMB sales teams manage leads, contacts, opportunities, and outreach workflows.
main_blocker: None

name: Copper
one_liner: Copper is a CRM built around Google Workspace that manages leads, contacts, companies, opportunities, pipelines, tasks, and projects for sales teams.
main_blocker: None for building (instant trial credentials); ongoing API use requires a Professional-or-above paid seat after the 14-day trial.

name: DealCloud
one_liner: Intapp DealCloud is a deal, pipeline, and relationship management (CRM) platform for capital-markets firms such as private equity, investment banking, and law firms.
primary_auth: OAuth2 client-credentials flow: a user-generated API key plu
main_blocker: No public API access without an existing Intapp DealCloud enterprise contract - no trial, sandbox, or self-serve signup path to credentials.

name: Zendesk
one_liner: Customer support and helpdesk platform for managing tickets, knowledge base, and omnichannel customer conversations.
main_blocker: None significant; API access is self-serve via trial or paid account. Only mild friction is the upcoming API-token deprecation pushing toward OAuth.

name: Intercom
one_liner: Customer communication and support platform combining live chat/messenger, help desk ticketing, help center content, and the Fin AI agent.
main_blocker: None

name: Freshdesk
one_liner: Cloud helpdesk/customer-support ticketing platform with a full-featured v2 REST API for tickets, contacts, agents, companies, and knowledge base.
main_blocker: None for core build: public REST API with self-serve API keys on free/trial accounts. Only caveat is the awkward Basic-auth convention (API key as username) and per-plan rate limits.

name: Front
one_liner: Front is a customer communication/shared-inbox helpdesk platform with a comprehensive REST Core API for managing conversations, contacts, messages, tags, and more.
main_blocker: None significant — public REST API with self-serve credentials. Minor gates: must be an admin to mint API tokens, and Front itself requires a paid plan/trial (no free-forever tier).

name: Pylon
one_liner: B2B customer support and helpdesk platform with a REST API for programmatically managing issues, accounts, contacts, users, teams, and knowledge bases.
main_blocker: API access requires a paid Pylon account and Admin privileges to mint tokens; no free/self-serve developer tier or public sandbox is documented.

name: LiveAgent
one_liner: Help desk and live chat support software with a REST API and a vendor-built MCP server for ticket, contact, and agent automation.
main_blocker: None significant; standard REST API with API-key auth and self-serve credentials. Webhook/event support is not documented, so real-time triggers would rely on polling.

name: Plain
one_liner: Plain is a customer support/helpdesk platform (email, Slack, live chat) built API-first with a GraphQL API for managing threads, customers, and tenants.
main_blocker: API access requires a Plain account; there is no permanently free tier, only a 7-day free trial (no credit card) after which a paid plan is needed to keep using the API.

name: Help Scout
one_liner: Shared inbox, help desk, and knowledge base platform for customer support teams.
main_blocker: None significant — public REST API with self-serve OAuth2/PAT credentials. Minor friction: requires a Help Scout account (trial or paid) and 48-hour token expiry requiring refresh.

name: Gorgias
one_liner: Ecommerce-focused customer support helpdesk that centralizes tickets, chat, and automation across channels.
main_blocker: Requires a Gorgias account (paid SaaS, free trial available); API credentials themselves are instant and self-serve once signed in.

name: Gladly
one_liner: Gladly is a customer service/helpdesk platform centered on people (customers) rather than tickets, with a REST API for customers, conversations, communications, tasks, and more.
primary_auth: Token-based HTTP Basic Authentication: agent email as userna
main_blocker: No public free/trial/sandbox path to credentials; you must be an existing paying Gladly customer with a user assigned the API User role to generate tokens.

name: Slack
one_liner: Team messaging and collaboration platform with an extensive HTTP API for building bots, apps, and agent integrations across channels and workspaces.
main_blocker: None for core messaging; a few admin/Enterprise Grid endpoints require paid enterprise plans, and OAuth app setup plus scopes/workspace install is a modest onboarding step.

name: Twilio
one_liner: Cloud communications platform providing programmable SMS, voice, WhatsApp, email, and verification APIs.
primary_auth: HTTP Basic auth using Account SID + Auth Token
main_blocker: None for building; trial accounts have sending restrictions (verified recipients only) until upgraded to a paid account.

name: Zoho Cliq
one_liner: Zoho Cliq is a team chat and collaboration app with a full REST API for messaging, channels, bots, and workflow automation.
main_blocker: None significant; standard OAuth 2.0 app registration in the Zoho API Console is required, which is self-serve and free.

name: Lark (Larksuite)
one_liner: Lark (Larksuite) is ByteDance's international all-in-one collaboration suite (messaging, docs, Base, calendar, video, approvals) with a broad Open Platform API for building apps and bots.
main_blocker: None for development: self-serve free app creation gives working credentials. Minor friction is that certain API scopes and app publication require workspace-admin approval.

name: Pumble
one_liner: Pumble is a free team chat and collaboration app (Slack alternative) that exposes a REST API via an installable API-keys addon for sending messages, managing channels, and reactions.
main_blocker: None significant: public REST API with self-serve API keys on any plan. Main limitation is narrow API surface (small set of messaging operations) and no documented webhooks/event subscriptions for inbound triggers.

name: Discord
one_liner: Chat/voice community platform whose developer API lets bots and apps read/send messages, manage guilds, channels, roles, and members, and stream real-time events.
main_blocker: None for building; free self-serve bot token and OAuth2 credentials. Only large/public bots (100+ servers) later hit a verification gate for privileged intents.

name: Telegram
one_liner: Cloud-based messaging platform offering a REST Bot API and the lower-level MTProto client API for building bots, userbots, and messaging integrations.
main_blocker: None significant; credentials are free and self-serve. Note the Bot API and MTProto are distinct surfaces, and MTProto usage is monitored for spam/abuse.

name: WhatsApp Business
one_liner: Meta's WhatsApp Business Platform lets businesses send and receive WhatsApp messages (templates, media, interactive) programmatically via the Cloud API built on the Graph API REST interface.
main_blocker: Test credentials are self-serve and free, but real production use requires Meta business verification, a verified business phone number, and app publishing approval, plus per-message billing.

name: Aircall
one_liner: Cloud-based business phone and call-center platform with a REST API for calls, contacts, numbers, SMS, and conversation intelligence.
main_blocker: API requires an active (paid or trial) Aircall account to generate credentials; no free-forever developer tier or open sandbox. OAuth partner flow is approval-gated, though own-account Basic Auth is self-serve.

name: Vonage
one_liner: Cloud communications platform offering programmable APIs for SMS, Voice, Verify (2FA), WhatsApp/Messages, Video, and Network APIs.
main_blocker: None for building; live production sending requires upgrading from the trial (trial can only reach the registered/verified test numbers), so end-to-end delivery beyond test numbers needs a paid account.

name: Google Ads
one_liner: Google's online advertising platform for search, display, video, and shopping ads, exposing a full-featured API to manage campaigns, budgets, bidding, and reporting programmatically.
main_blocker: Developer token Basic Access approval (~5 business days) is required before the API works against real advertiser accounts; only test accounts are self-serve. Auth also requires combining OAuth2 tokens with a developer token and login-customer-id headers.

name: Meta Ads
one_liner: Meta's Marketing API (part of the Facebook Graph API) lets developers programmatically create, manage, and report on advertising campaigns across Facebook, Instagram, and Meta's ad network.
main_blocker: Production API access is gated behind App Review, Business Verification, and Advanced Access approval; the instant self-serve dev tier is too throttled for real workloads.

name: LinkedIn Ads
one_liner: LinkedIn's Marketing/Advertising API lets partners programmatically create and manage ad accounts, campaigns, creatives, targeting, and pull performance analytics from LinkedIn Campaign Manager.
main_blocker: Advertising API access is gated behind LinkedIn Marketing Partner approval; a plain developer app cannot call the Ads API without going through the partner/product approval process.

name: GoHighLevel
one_liner: GoHighLevel (HighLevel) is an all-in-one CRM, marketing, and sales automation platform for agencies, exposing a REST API v2 covering contacts, conversations, calendars, opportunities, payments, and more.
main_blocker: None major: public REST API with self-serve OAuth app creation. Only friction is needing an active HighLevel account (paid SaaS, trial available) to authorize and obtain tokens.

name: Mailchimp
one_liner: Email marketing and automation platform whose Marketing API lets agents manage audiences, campaigns, automations, reports, and e-commerce data.
main_blocker: None significant; API is public with self-serve credentials. Available operations scale with account plan level, and data-center prefix must be derived from the key.

name: Klaviyo
one_liner: Klaviyo is a marketing automation platform for email, SMS, and customer data, with a comprehensive REST API for managing profiles, campaigns, flows, events, and catalogs.
main_blocker: None significant. Free self-serve account provides working API credentials immediately.

name: systeme.io
one_liner: All-in-one marketing platform (sales funnels, email marketing, courses, affiliate programs) offering a REST public API to manage contacts, tags, funnels, newsletters, courses, and webhooks.
main_blocker: None significant - public REST API with self-serve API keys available (likely including the free plan). Breadth is moderate rather than broad.

name: Pinterest
one_liner: Pinterest's v5 REST API lets apps manage organic content (pins, boards, catalogs), run and analyze ads, and pull analytics on behalf of authenticated Pinterest users.
main_blocker: Multi-user / production API use requires Pinterest app review and Standard Access approval; trial-mode credentials only work against the app owner's own account.

name: Threads (Meta)
one_liner: Meta's Threads API lets apps publish posts, manage replies/mentions, read insights, and search on the Threads social network on behalf of Threads users.
main_blocker: Self-serve free credentials for own account/testers; the real gate is Meta App Review + app publishing required before non-tester end users can authorize the app for production use.

name: SendGrid
one_liner: Twilio SendGrid is a cloud email delivery platform for transactional and marketing email via a REST Web API v3 and SMTP relay.
main_blocker: No hard gate for building: public REST API with self-serve API keys. Only caveat is the free tier is now a 60-day trial, so long-term production use requires a paid plan.

name: Shopify
one_liner: Leading commerce platform whose Admin API lets apps read and manage stores' products, orders, customers, inventory, and fulfillment.
main_blocker: None for building/testing: free Partner account + dev store yields instant API tokens. Only friction is that live merchant stores require a paid plan, and public app distribution requires app review for the App Store.

name: WooCommerce
one_liner: Open-source WooCommerce is a WordPress e-commerce plugin whose built-in REST API exposes store data (products, orders, customers, coupons) to external systems.
main_blocker: No API-side gate; the only prerequisite is having a WooCommerce store instance to generate keys against (no hosted/central API - each merchant's own site is the endpoint).

name: BigCommerce
one_liner: SaaS ecommerce platform for building online stores, with a comprehensive REST/GraphQL Admin API for managing catalog, orders, customers, and storefront.
primary_auth: OAuth2 access token passed via the X-Auth-Token header
main_blocker: None significant: public API with self-serve OAuth credentials from a free sandbox/store control panel. Partner registration is required to create a free sandbox but is self-serve.

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
one_liner: Enterprise SaaS ecommerce platform (B2C/B2B Commerce) with REST APIs (SCAPI and legacy OCAPI) for shopper storefront and admin/catalog/order operations.
main_blocker: No self-serve API credentials: you need a provisioned B2C Commerce instance and Account Manager/SLAS access available only to existing customers or Salesforce partners. Once instance credentials exist, the REST APIs are standard OAuth2/JWT and straightforward to integrate.

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
one_liner: Open-source and enterprise ecommerce platform (Magento Open Source / Adobe Commerce) exposing REST, GraphQL, and SOAP web APIs for managing catalog, orders, customers, carts, and inventory.
main_blocker: No hard gate for the API itself: on a self-hosted Magento Open Source or any Adobe Commerce instance you create integration tokens directly. The main friction is that each integration targets a specific merchant's own instance (no central Adobe-hosted API/sandbox with instant creds), and Adobe Commerce enterprise features require a paid license.

name: Squarespace
one_liner: Website and ecommerce platform whose Commerce APIs let developers manage orders, products, inventory, transactions, and customer profiles on Squarespace online stores.
main_blocker: API access is gated behind a paid Squarespace plan (Commerce Advanced for custom-app Commerce API keys), and OAuth for commercial Extensions requires an application form/approval; no free credential path confirmed.

name: Ecwid
one_liner: Ecwid (by Lightspeed) is a hosted ecommerce platform that lets merchants add an online store to any website or social channel, with a REST API for managing products, orders, customers and store settings.
main_blocker: None significant; single-store secret token is trivially obtained but public multi-store distribution requires the OAuth app-registration/install flow.

name: Gumroad
one_liner: Creator-focused ecommerce platform for selling digital products, memberships, and subscriptions, with a REST API for products, sales, subscribers, and license verification.
main_blocker: None significant. Self-serve access tokens make integration straightforward; only minor friction is that OAuth for third-party multi-account apps requires registering an application.

name: Amazon Selling Partner
one_liner: Amazon's Selling Partner API (SP-API) lets sellers and solution providers programmatically manage orders, inventory, listings, fulfillment (FBA), finances, and reports across Amazon marketplaces.
main_blocker: Registration/approval gate: needs a Professional Amazon seller account, developer profile review, role qualification, and (for PII roles) an architecture review before production API access is granted.

name: fanbasis
one_liner: FanBasis is full-stack business infrastructure for internet creators/entrepreneurs, unifying payments, checkout, subscriptions, courses/community, and CRM, with a REST API for payment and commerce operations.
main_blocker: Developer docs portal is login-gated and API keys are provisioned from an account dashboard; unclear whether all self-serve accounts get instant live API access or require sales onboarding (enterprises page emphasizes a discovery call). A sandbox exists, so building/testing is feasible once an account is created.

name: DataForSEO
one_liner: Pay-as-you-go SEO and web-scraping data provider exposing SERP, keyword, backlink, on-page, and marketplace data via a unified REST/JSON API.
main_blocker: No hard gate; self-serve credentials work immediately. Only friction is prepaid billing ($50 minimum) after the limited free trial, and task-based endpoints require polling or webhook callbacks.

name: SE Ranking
one_liner: SE Ranking is an SEO platform whose Data and Project APIs expose keyword research, rank tracking, backlinks, domain analysis, site audits, AI search visibility, and project management data over REST.
primary_auth: API key sent in the HTTP Authorization header using the Toke
main_blocker: No permanent free API tier: working credentials require starting a 14-day trial (instant, no approval) or a paid/standalone plan, and usage is metered by credits.

name: Ahrefs
one_liner: SEO toolset whose API v3 exposes Site Explorer, Keywords Explorer, backlinks, rank tracker and site audit data for agents.
main_blocker: API requires a paid Lite+ subscription (no trial/sandbox path to working credentials); only limited free test queries are available.

name: MrScraper
one_liner: AI-powered web scraping platform with pre-built marketplace scrapers and a REST API for extracting structured data from any website.
main_blocker: None significant; free tier provides working API credentials self-serve. Token budget on free tier is limited (1,000 tokens).

name: Apify
one_liner: Cloud platform for web scraping and browser automation, running thousands of prebuilt 'Actors' (scrapers/crawlers) and storing their results, all accessible via a REST API.
main_blocker: None significant. Free self-serve API token; the main practical consideration is that heavy scraping consumes platform usage credits (pay-per-use / compute units), so production-scale usage requires a paid plan, but the API itself is fully usable on the free tier.

name: Firecrawl
one_liner: Web scraping and crawling API that turns websites into clean, LLM-ready markdown, HTML, or structured JSON via scrape, crawl, map, search, and extract endpoints.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve API key available instantly, though free tier is credit-limited (1,000/month) and heavy use requires a paid plan.

name: Bright Data
one_liner: Web data platform offering proxy networks and scraping APIs (Web Unlocker, SERP, Scraping Browser, Web Scraper API, and dataset marketplace) for collecting public web data at scale.
main_blocker: None significant for building an integration; self-serve API key with a free tier is available. Cost/credit consumption is the main practical consideration at scale.

name: Sherlock
one_liner: Open-source OSINT CLI tool that hunts down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks.
main_blocker: None for local use. It is a local CLI / self-hosted tool rather than a hosted SaaS API, so integration means wrapping the CLI or self-hosting the REST API; there is no vendor-hosted endpoint to call.

name: Waterfall.io
one_liner: B2B contact/company data enrichment API that aggregates 30+ data vendors into a unified schema (prospecting, contact/phone/company enrichment, email verification).
main_blocker: No self-serve credential path — API keys are provisioned via a sales/demo conversation and pricing is unpublished, so a build cannot obtain working credentials without vendor contact.

name: Clay
one_liner: GTM data platform that finds and enriches company/people records via 150+ data providers, now exposed through a public REST API, CLI, and agent plugin.
main_blocker: Meaningful API usage consumes Actions/Data Credits; the Free plan's ~100 credits/month is enough to test but real workloads require a paid plan, and Tables queries are Enterprise-gated.

name: GitHub
one_liner: GitHub is the leading developer platform for hosting Git repositories, code collaboration, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD, all exposed through a comprehensive public API.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and broad public API. Rate limits and GitHub App installation flows are the main operational considerations.

name: Vercel
one_liner: Vercel is a frontend cloud / deployment platform whose REST API lets agents manage deployments, projects, domains, DNS, environment variables, teams, and webhooks.
main_blocker: None significant. Public REST API with instant free self-serve tokens; only friction is team-scoped resources needing an explicit teamId parameter.

name: Netlify
one_liner: Netlify is a cloud platform for building, deploying, and hosting web apps and serverless functions, with a REST API for managing sites, deploys, env vars, forms, and hooks.
main_blocker: None significant. Public REST API with self-serve free credentials (PAT). Deploy rate limits (100/day) could constrain high-volume deploy automation.

name: Cloudflare
one_liner: Cloudflare provides CDN, DNS, security, edge compute (Workers) and developer platform services, all manageable via a broad public REST API.
main_blocker: None significant. Free account yields working scoped API tokens instantly; the only friction is choosing appropriate token scopes for the target products.

name: Supabase
one_liner: Open-source Firebase alternative providing a hosted Postgres database with auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs, auth, storage, edge functions, and a project Management API.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and public REST/GraphQL APIs. Data-plane auth is per-project (project URL + keys) rather than a single global API, so an integration must handle per-project credentials.

name: Neo4j
one_liner: Neo4j is a graph database platform (self-hosted and Aura cloud) that exposes a REST management API for Aura instances, an HTTP Query API for running Cypher, and language drivers over the Bolt protocol.
main_blocker: None significant for building agent tools: free tier gives working credentials, the Query API runs Cypher over HTTP with basic auth, and the Aura API uses standard OAuth2 client-credentials. Minor friction: production database access is idiomatically via Bolt drivers (SDK) rather than REST, and creating paid instances via the management API needs billing info on file.

name: Snowflake
one_liner: Snowflake is a cloud data platform (AI Data Cloud) for data warehousing, analytics, and AI, exposing SQL and resource-management REST APIs plus a managed MCP server.
main_blocker: None hard: public REST/SQL APIs with self-serve trial creds (no card). Minor friction is key-pair/JWT setup and that sustained use beyond the 30-day/$400 trial requires a paid account.

name: MongoDB Atlas
one_liner: Fully-managed cloud database (DBaaS) with a comprehensive REST Administration API for programmatically managing clusters, projects, users, backups, billing, and monitoring.
main_blocker: None significant. Creds are self-serve free. Minor friction: legacy API keys use HTTP Digest auth (less common), though the modern OAuth2 service-account flow is standard. Optional IP access lists can gate token usage if enabled.

name: Datadog
one_liner: Cloud observability and monitoring platform (metrics, traces, logs, dashboards, monitors, incidents) with a comprehensive HTTP REST API.
main_blocker: None significant. Dual-key auth (API key + application key) is slightly more involved than a single token, and application key secrets cannot be retrieved after creation, but self-serve credentials are available instantly on a free/trial account.

name: Sentry
one_liner: Application error monitoring and performance tracing platform with a comprehensive REST API for issues, events, projects, releases, alerts, and crons.
main_blocker: None significant - public REST API with self-serve free-tier credentials. Only nuance is region-specific base URLs.

name: Notion
one_liner: Connected workspace for notes, docs, wikis, and databases; its REST API lets agents read and write pages, databases, blocks, comments, and files.
main_blocker: None significant for self-serve; only caveat is PAT creation being disabled by default on Business/Enterprise plans (internal integration tokens and OAuth remain available).

name: Airtable
one_liner: Cloud spreadsheet-database hybrid for building relational apps, with a REST Web API to read/write records, manage schema, and subscribe to change webhooks.
main_blocker: None significant. Free self-serve personal access tokens plus a documented REST API and webhooks make it straightforward to build agent tools.

name: Linear
one_liner: Linear is a project and issue tracking tool for software teams, exposing a comprehensive GraphQL API plus a TypeScript SDK for programmatic access to issues, projects, comments, cycles, and more.
main_blocker: None significant. API is public and credentials are self-serve/free. Only minor consideration is that the API is GraphQL-only, so integrations must speak GraphQL rather than REST.

name: Jira
one_liner: Atlassian's issue tracking and project management platform with a broad Cloud REST API for issues, projects, workflows, and boards.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and a broad public REST API. Minor: API tokens now expire (default 1 year) so long-lived integrations should use OAuth.

name: Asana
one_liner: Work management and project-tracking SaaS with a broad public REST API (the Asana Work Graph) for tasks, projects, portfolios, users, and workflows.
main_blocker: None significant. Public REST API with self-serve free credentials (PAT or OAuth). Only minor consideration is choosing OAuth for multi-tenant apps.

name: Monday.com
one_liner: Work OS / project management platform whose GraphQL Platform API lets agents read and modify boards, items, columns, users, workspaces, docs, and more.
main_blocker: None material. API access is free and self-serve via personal token; only friction is complexity-based rate limits with a paid add-on for high-volume usage.

name: ClickUp
one_liner: Project management and productivity platform (tasks, docs, goals, time tracking) with a broad public REST API and a first-party MCP server.
main_blocker: None significant - public REST API with self-serve free credentials. Only caveat is per-plan rate limits.

name: Coda
one_liner: Coda is an all-in-one doc/workspace platform (docs that combine text, tables, and apps); its REST API lets you read and write docs, tables, rows, pages, formulas, controls, and trigger automations.
main_blocker: None significant. Self-serve token is instant and free. Minor considerations: asynchronous write model (202 + request IDs) and moderate rate limits. Note Coda was acquired and docs now redirect to docs.superhuman.com, though the API host remains coda.io/apis/v1.

name: Smartsheet
one_liner: Enterprise work-management and project collaboration platform (spreadsheet-style sheets, reports, dashboards, and automation) with a REST API for programmatic access.
main_blocker: API access is gated behind a paid Business/Enterprise/Advanced plan; trial accounts cannot generate API tokens, so there is no free self-serve credential path. Once on a qualifying plan, the REST API is broad, well-documented, and easy to build against.

name: Harvest
one_liner: Time tracking, invoicing, and project-budget SaaS for teams, with a full REST API v2 covering time entries, projects, clients, invoices, estimates, expenses, and users.
main_blocker: None significant; personal access tokens are instantly self-serve on any account. Only minor friction is that personal tokens require passing both the token and a Harvest-Account-Id header.

name: Stripe
one_liner: Payments platform whose REST API lets developers accept payments and manage customers, subscriptions, invoices, payouts, and more.
main_blocker: None for building/testing: public REST API with self-serve test keys. Going live with real payments requires account activation/business verification, but development and integration need no approval.

name: Plaid
one_liner: Financial data network that connects apps to users' bank accounts for balances, transactions, identity, auth, payments, and KYC/underwriting data.
main_blocker: Sandbox keys free+instant; only Production access needs approval

name: Binance
one_liner: World's largest cryptocurrency exchange offering spot, futures, margin, options, and wallet trading via a broad public REST/WebSocket/FIX API.
main_blocker: None significant for building: public documented API with self-serve testnet keys. Request signing (HMAC/RSA/Ed25519) via X-MBX-APIKEY header adds mild complexity, and production trading requires a verified account.

name: Paygent Connect
one_liner: Paygent Connect is a white-label, NMI-powered payment gateway for merchants/ISOs to process cards and manage transactions, exposing NMI's Payment API for integration.
main_blocker: Credentials are gated: no self-serve signup for the branded gateway, and a live per-merchant NMI security_key requires a merchant account provisioned via the ISO/reseller. The API itself (NMI) is well-documented and integration-friendly once you have keys.

name: iPayX
one_liner: Rail-agnostic FX forensic audit layer for B2B payments that scores foreign-exchange transactions and detects hidden bank/card-network markups against live mid-market rates across 300+ currencies.
main_blocker: None major for basic use: free-tier API keys are self-serve from the dashboard and some tools need no auth. Full forensic reports require a paid (~$199) tier. Note the /docs and REST reference pages returned 404 at time of research, so the full REST surface is not fully verifiable.

name: QuickBooks
one_liner: Intuit's QuickBooks Online exposes a REST Accounting API covering dozens of accounting entities (invoices, customers, bills, payments, reports) via OAuth 2.0, with a free sandbox and an official Intuit-published MCP server.
main_blocker: None for building/testing: free dev account + sandbox gives working OAuth credentials. Minor friction is OAuth2 authorization-code flow with hourly token refresh, and moving to production requires connecting a real QBO company.

name: Xero
one_liner: Cloud accounting platform for small businesses whose API exposes invoices, contacts, bank transactions, payroll and financial reports as agent-callable operations.
main_blocker: None significant for building; production scale requires app certification to lift the uncertified-app connection limit, but development and testing are fully self-serve free.

name: Brex
one_liner: Corporate card, banking, expense management, and bill pay platform for businesses, exposed via a REST API and an official MCP server.
main_blocker: Requires an approved Brex business account to obtain any real API credentials; partner OAuth apps additionally require contacting Brex developer support. No open self-serve sandbox for outside developers.

name: Ramp
one_liner: Corporate card and spend-management platform whose Developer API exposes cards, transactions, bills, reimbursements, users, and accounting sync for agentic finance automation.
main_blocker: None for core build: public REST API with self-serve credentials and sandbox. Only elevated card-vault (full PAN/CVV) access requires PCI qualification via support ticket.

name: PitchBook
one_liner: Private capital market intelligence platform (companies, deals, investors, funds, LPs) with a RESTful Direct Data API for pulling PitchBook data on demand.
main_blocker: API access requires a separate paid contract and an account-manager-issued sandbox/production key; no self-serve or public credentials. MCP connector is gated behind enterprise/partner PitchBook licenses.

name: NotebookLM
one_liner: Google's AI research-and-writing assistant that grounds summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews in your uploaded documents; the NotebookLM Enterprise edition on Google Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic notebook and source management.
main_blocker: A real public REST API exists but only in the Enterprise edition, which requires a licensed Google Cloud / Gemini Enterprise setup plus IAM roles - not self-serve. Consumer NotebookLM has no official API.

name: Otter AI
one_liner: AI meeting assistant that records, transcribes, summarizes, and surfaces action items from voice conversations and meetings.
main_blocker: No public REST API to wrap; the only official programmatic path is Otter's own hosted OAuth MCP server (read-only transcript/summary/action-item access), so a Composio-style tool would ride on that MCP rather than a documented HTTP API.

name: Fathom
one_liner: AI meeting notetaker that records, transcribes, and summarizes calls; its public REST API exposes meeting summaries, transcripts, action items, and webhooks for agent workflows.
main_blocker: None significant for the REST API: self-serve API key, no approval. Minor caveat is uncertainty over whether API access is limited to certain plan tiers, and the official MCP server appears paid-only.

name: Consensus
one_liner: AI-powered academic search engine over 200M+ peer-reviewed papers, exposing a REST 'quick search' API and an official remote MCP server for grounding agents in scientific evidence.
main_blocker: REST API is application-gated (approval + paid per-call pricing), so working credentials are not self-serve; however the official OAuth-based remote MCP offers a self-serve alternative path.

name: Reducto
one_liner: Agentic document platform providing layout-aware OCR, parsing, extraction, splitting, and editing APIs that turn complex real-world documents into LLM-ready structured output.
main_blocker: None significant — public REST API with self-serve free account and API keys. Exact free-tier limits are unspecified.

name: Devin
one_liner: Devin (by Cognition) is an autonomous AI software engineer that plans, writes, tests, and ships code; it exposes a REST API to create and manage agent sessions, knowledge, playbooks, and secrets.
primary_auth: Service-account API key (cog_ prefix; PATs coming)
main_blocker: API access requires a paid Team/Teams or Enterprise plan; no free tier or sandbox issues working API credentials, and API usage consumes billable agent-compute credits (ACUs).

name: higgsfield
one_liner: Higgsfield is an AI media-generation platform (image, video, 3D, audio across 100+ models) exposing a Cloud REST API, official Python/Node SDKs, a CLI, and an MCP server for agent-driven generation.
main_blocker: None blocking for building tools: public REST API with self-serve API keys, official SDKs, webhooks, and an official MCP server. Only practical constraint is credit consumption per generation.

name: Mermaid CLI
one_liner: Open-source command-line tool (mmdc) that renders Mermaid diagram definitions into SVG/PNG/PDF; distributed as an npm package and Docker image under MIT license.
main_blocker: No HTTP API — integration is via local CLI/Node API rather than a hosted service, so a Composio tool would need to shell out to mmdc (requires Node/Chromium/Puppeteer runtime) rather than call a remote endpoint.

name: YouTube Transcript
one_liner: TranscriptAPI (transcriptapi.com) is a hosted REST API for extracting YouTube video transcripts plus searching videos/channels and browsing channels and playlists.
main_blocker: None significant; free tier is capped at 100 credits so sustained use requires a low-cost paid plan, but building/testing a tool is fully self-serve.

name: Grain
one_liner: AI meeting-notetaker that records, transcribes, and summarizes calls, with a REST API exposing recordings, transcripts, notes, action items, deals, and coaching data.
main_blocker: None for core meeting/transcript/notes data - self-serve tokens on any plan. Deals and sales-coaching endpoints require a Business/Enterprise plan.
